fields.name: Schedule Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the unique name (code) of the financial report row definition. You can use up to 10 characters.

fields.name: Line No.
fields.tooltip:
Shows the number of the account schedule line.
The program fills in the field automatically and uses the number to keep track of the various lines in the account schedule.

fields.name: Row No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a number that identifies the line.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies text that will appear on the financial report line.

fields.name: Totaling
fields.tooltip:
Specifies which accounts will be totaled on this line.
How you fill out this field depends on the contents of the Totaling Type field. If you assign the Formula totaling type to the account schedule line, you can enter a formula that includes row numbers (or totaling intervals) from the account schedule. If the totaling type is Posting Accounts or Total Accounts, you must enter an account number (or totaling interval) from the chart of accounts.
When you use a totaling interval, all the accounts within the range you define (for example, 1900..2100 inclusive) will be totaled.
You can use a formula to perform calculations on other lines. Other lines are referenced through their row number.
You can use the following symbols:
+ (addition)
- (subtraction)
* (multiplication)
/ (division)
^ (exponentiation)
() (parentheses)
% (percent) - If used between two terms, the result is the same as for / (division), but multiplied by 100.

fields.name: Totaling Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the totaling type for the financial report line. The type determines which accounts within the totaling interval you specify in the Totaling field will be totaled.

fields.name: New Page
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether there will be a page break after the current line when the financial report is exported to PDF or printed.

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the date interval that values are filtered by.
If this field contains starting and ending dates for a date interval, then other field values only show information for that interval.
You can enter a date interval according to the following rules.


Meaning


Sample expression


Entries that are included


Equal to

12 15 12

Only entries that are posted on December 15, 2012.


Interval

12 15 12..01 15 13
..12 15 12

Entries that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and January 15, 2013.
Entries that are posted on January 15, 2012, or earlier.


Either/or

12 15 12|12 16 12

Entries that are posted on either December 15, 2012, or December 16, 2012. If there are entries that are posted on both days, then all entries are displayed.


You can also combine the various format types.


Sample expression


Entries that are included


12 15 12|12 01 12..12 10 12

Entries that are posted either on December 15, 2012, or on dates between and including December 1, 2012, and December 10, 2012.


..12 14 12|12 30 12..

Entries that are posted on December 14, 2012, or earlier, or entries that are posted on December 30, 2012, or later, which includes all entries except those that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and December 29, 2012.

fields.name: Dimension 1 Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the filter to use on analysis view entries in an account schedule. If the Analysis View Name field in the Account Schedule window is blank, you can enter in the Dimension 1 Filter field a Global Dimension 1 value code here that the account schedule line will be linked to.
If you have entered a code in the Analysis View Code field in the Account Schedule window, you can select a value code from those available for Dimension 1 in the selected analysis view. The account schedule line will then be linked to that dimension value.
When you specify the dimension value code, you determine whether accounts for specific dimension values will be included in the printout and the account schedule overview.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Dimension 2 Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the filter to use on analysis view entries in an account schedule. If the Analysis View Name field in the Account Schedule window is blank, you can enter in the Dimension 2 Filter field a Global Dimension 2 value code here that the account schedule line will be linked to.
If you have entered a code in the Analysis View Code field in the Account Schedule window, you can select a value code from those available for Dimension 2 in the selected analysis view. The account schedule line will then be linked to that dimension value.
In specifying the dimension value code, you determine whether accounts for specific dimension values will be included in the printout and the account schedule overview.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: G/L Budget Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a code for a general ledger budget that the account schedule line will be filtered on.
The G/L budget code determines whether accounts from specific G/L budgets will be included in the printout.

fields.name: Business Unit Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a code for a business unit that the account schedule line will be linked to.
The business unit code determines whether accounts from specific business units will be included in the printout.

fields.name: Show
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the line will be printed on the report.

fields.name: Dimension 3 Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the filter to use on analysis view entries in an account schedule. If you have entered a name in the Analysis View Name field in the Account Schedule window, you can enter a value code here from those available for Dimension 3 in the selected analysis view. The account schedule line will then be linked to that dimension value.
To see the dimension value codes available for Dimension 3 in the Analysis View table, choose the filter field.
In specifying the dimension value code, you determine whether accounts for specific dimension values will be included in the printout and the account schedule overview.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Dimension 4 Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the filter to use on analysis view entries in an account schedule. If you have entered a name in the Analysis View Name field in the Account Schedule window, you can enter a value code here from those available for Dimension 4 in the selected analysis view. The account schedule line will then be linked to that dimension value.
To see the dimension value codes available for Dimension 4 in the Analysis View table, choose the filter field.
In specifying the dimension value code, you determine whether accounts for specific dimension values will be included in the printout and the account schedule overview.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Dimension 1 Totaling
fields.tooltip: Specifies which dimension value amounts will be totaled on this line.

fields.name: Dimension 2 Totaling
fields.tooltip: Specifies which dimension value amounts will be totaled on this line.

fields.name: Dimension 3 Totaling
fields.tooltip: Specifies which dimension value amounts will be totaled on this line.

fields.name: Dimension 4 Totaling
fields.tooltip: Specifies which dimension value amounts will be totaled on this line.

fields.name: Bold
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to print the amounts in this row in bold.

fields.name: Italic
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to print the amounts in this row in italics.

fields.name: Underline
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to underline the amounts in this row.

fields.name: Show Opposite Sign
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to show debits in reports as negative amounts with a minus sign and credits as positive amounts.

fields.name: Row Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the row type for the row definition. The type determines how the amounts in the row are calculated.

fields.name: Amount Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of entries that will be included in the amounts in the row definition.

fields.name: Double Underline
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to double underline the amounts in this row.

fields.name: Hide Currency Symbol
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to hide currency symbols when a calculated result is not a currency.

fields.name: Internal Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies the internal description for the row definition line that highlights complex logic or areas needing review for the next report designer.

fields.name: Cash Flow Forecast Filter
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Cost Center Filter
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Cost Object Filter
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Cost Center Totaling
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Cost Object Totaling
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Cost Budget Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a code for a cost budget that the account schedule line will be filtered on.
The cost budget code determines whether cost types from specific cost budgets will be included in the printout.
